sections.title: Physics - Section A
sections.description: Compulsory section. Attempt all 45 questions. +4 for correct, -1 for incorrect.

questions.text: Two point charges +q and +4q are placed at a distance r apart. The ratio of forces experienced by the two charges is:
questions.explanation: By Newton's third law, the forces on both charges are equal and opposite, so the ratio is 1:1.

questions.text: A uniformly charged sphere of radius R has total charge Q. The electric field at an interior point at distance r (r < R) from the centre is:
questions.explanation: By Gauss's law, E = Qr / 4πε₀R³ inside a uniformly charged sphere.

questions.text: The work done in moving a charge q from point A to B, both lying on the same equipotential surface of potential V, is:
questions.explanation: No work is done in moving a charge along an equipotential surface since potential difference is zero.

questions.text: Three capacitors of capacitances 2 μF, 3 μF, and 6 μF are connected in series. The equivalent capacitance is:
A parallel plate capacitor with air has capacitance C. If the entire space is filled with dielectric of constant K = 4 and plate separation is doubled, the new capacitance is:
questions.explanation: C_new = Kε₀A/(2d) = 4 × (ε₀A/d) / 2 = 4C/2 = 2C.

questions.text: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.explanation: Energy = ½CV².

questions.text: A wire of resistance 4 Ω is bent into a circle. The effective resistance between the two ends of a diameter is:
questions.explanation: Each semicircle has resistance 2 Ω. They are in parallel: R = (2×2)/(2+2) = 1 Ω.

questions.text: A cell of EMF 2 V and internal resistance 0.5 Ω is connected to an external resistance of 1.5 Ω. The terminal voltage of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Current I = E/(r+R) = 2/(0.5+1.5) = 1 A. Terminal voltage = E − Ir = 2 − 1×0.5 = 1.5 V.

questions.text: Two cells with EMF E₁ = 2 V, internal resistance r₁ = 1 Ω and E₂ = 4 V, r₂ = 2 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent EMF of the combination is:
In a Wheatstone bridge, the condition for balance (no current through galvanometer) is:
questions.explanation: The Wheatstone bridge is balanced when P/Q = R/S, so no current flows through the galvanometer.

questions.text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop of radius r carrying current I is:
questions.explanation: B = μ₀I/2r at the centre of a circular loop.

questions.text: A charged particle enters a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to it. The path traced by the particle is:
questions.explanation: The magnetic force is always perpendicular to velocity, providing centripetal acceleration; the path is circular.

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic flux is:
questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is measured in Weber (Wb). 1 Wb = 1 T·m².

questions.text: The torque experienced by a magnetic dipole of moment M in a uniform magnetic field B at angle θ is:
questions.explanation: Torque τ = MB sinθ (or vectorially τ = M × B).

questions.text: According to Faraday's law, the magnitude of induced EMF in a coil is proportional to:

options.text: Rate of change of magnetic flux

questions.explanation: Faraday's law: |EMF| = |dΦ/dt|, i.e., proportional to rate of change of magnetic flux.

questions.text: The mutual inductance between two coils is 1 H. If the current in one coil changes at a rate of 3 A/s, the EMF induced in the other coil is:
questions.explanation: EMF = M × (dI/dt) = 1 × 3 = 3 V.

questions.text: In a series LCR AC circuit, the power factor is:
questions.explanation: Power factor = cos φ = R/Z, where Z is the total impedance.

questions.text: At resonance in a series LCR circuit:
questions.explanation: At resonance, XL = XC, impedance Z = R (minimum), current is maximum, and power factor = 1.

questions.text: A ray of light passes from glass (n = 1.5) to water (n = 1.33). The critical angle for total internal reflection is approximately:
An object is placed 20 cm in front of a concave mirror of focal length 15 cm. The image distance is:
questions.explanation: Using 1/v + 1/u = 1/f with u = −20, f = −15: 1/v = −1/15 + 1/20 = −1/60. So v = −60 cm (real, in front of mirror).

questions.text: In Young's double slit experiment, slit separation is 1 mm, wavelength is 500 nm, and the screen is 1 m away. The fringe width is:
In Young's double slit experiment, if the wavelength of light is halved and the slit separation is doubled, the fringe width becomes:
questions.explanation: β = λD/d. New β = (λ/2)D/(2d) = β/4.

questions.text: A convex lens of focal length 10 cm forms a real, inverted, magnified image. The object is placed at:
questions.explanation: A real, inverted, magnified image is formed when the object is between f and 2f.

questions.text: The first law of thermodynamics is essentially a statement of:
questions.explanation: The first law (ΔU = Q − W) is conservation of energy applied to thermodynamic systems.

questions.text: For an ideal gas undergoing an adiabatic process:
questions.explanation: In an adiabatic process, no heat exchange occurs with the surroundings (ΔQ = 0).

questions.text: A Carnot engine operates between a source at 500 K and a sink at 375 K. Its efficiency is:
For an isothermal expansion of an ideal gas, the change in internal energy (ΔU) is:
questions.explanation: For an ideal gas, internal energy depends only on temperature. Isothermal means constant T, so ΔU = 0.

questions.text: The ratio of specific heats (γ = Cp/Cv) for a diatomic ideal gas is:
questions.explanation: For a diatomic gas, Cv = 5R/2, Cp = 7R/2, so γ = 7/5 = 1.4.

questions.text: In simple harmonic motion, the velocity of the particle is maximum at the:
questions.explanation: In SHM, v = ω√(A²−x²). Velocity is maximum when x = 0 (mean position).

questions.text: The time period of a simple pendulum is 2 s on Earth (g = 10 m/s²). Its length is approximately:
A projectile is launched at 45° with initial speed v₀. The maximum height reached is:
questions.explanation: H = v₀²sin²θ/(2g) = v₀²×(1/2)/(2g) = v₀²/4g.

questions.text: The angular momentum of a planet revolving around the sun is conserved because the gravitational force is:

options.text: A central force (directed along the line joining the two bodies)

questions.explanation: Gravitational force is a central force (radial), so torque about the sun is zero, and angular momentum is conserved.

questions.text: The escape velocity from the surface of Earth (radius R, gravitational acceleration g) is:
questions.explanation: Escape velocity v_e = √(2GM/R) = √(2gR) ≈ 11.2 km/s for Earth.

questions.text: The orbital velocity of a satellite at height h above Earth's surface (Earth's radius R, surface gravity g) is:
questions.explanation: v₀ = √(GM/(R+h)) = √(gR²/(R+h)), since GM = gR².

questions.text: The wave nature of electrons was experimentally verified by:
questions.explanation: Davisson and Germer (1927) demonstrated electron diffraction by a nickel crystal, confirming wave nature of electrons.

questions.text: The de Broglie wavelength of a particle of mass m moving with velocity v is:
questions.explanation: de Broglie wavelength λ = h/p = h/mv.

questions.text: A photon of frequency ν has energy:
questions.explanation: Energy of a photon E = hν, where h is Planck's constant.

questions.text: In the Bohr model of hydrogen atom, the radius of the nth orbit is proportional to:
questions.explanation: rₙ = n²a₀, where a₀ = 0.529 Å is the Bohr radius. Radius is proportional to n².

questions.text: The energy of an electron in the nth orbit of a hydrogen atom is:
questions.explanation: Eₙ = −13.6/n² eV. For n = 1 (ground state), E₁ = −13.6 eV.

questions.text: The nuclear radius R is related to mass number A by:
questions.explanation: R = R₀A^(1/3), where R₀ ≈ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁵ m.

questions.text: In a p-n junction diode under forward bias:
questions.explanation: Forward bias reduces barrier potential and narrows depletion region, allowing current to flow easily.

questions.text: In a common emitter npn transistor amplifier, the current gain β is defined as:
questions.explanation: β (DC current gain) = IC / IB in common emitter configuration.

questions.text: The logic gate that gives output HIGH (1) only when all inputs are LOW (0) is:
questions.explanation: NOR gate output is 1 only when all inputs are 0. It is a universal gate.

questions.text: The frequency range of X-rays in the electromagnetic spectrum is approximately:
questions.explanation: X-rays have frequencies in the range 10¹⁶ to 10²⁰ Hz, approximately 10¹⁸ Hz.

sections.title: Physics - Section B
sections.description: Attempt any 10 out of 15 questions. Only your best 10 answers will be scored.

questions.text: A rod of length L rotates in a plane about one end with angular velocity ω. If a uniform magnetic field B exists perpendicular to the plane of rotation, the EMF induced is:
questions.explanation: EMF = ∫₀ᴸ Bω x dx = BωL²/2.

questions.text: A step-down transformer has 500 turns in the primary and 100 turns in the secondary. If the primary voltage is 220 V, the secondary voltage is:
The wavelength range of visible light is approximately:
questions.explanation: Visible light spans approximately 400 nm (violet) to 700 nm (red).

questions.text: In Compton scattering, the scattered X-ray has a wavelength _____ the incident X-ray:
questions.explanation: Compton shift Δλ = (h/mₑc)(1−cosφ) ≥ 0. Scattered photon always has longer wavelength.

questions.text: Nuclear fission of ²³⁵U is most effectively initiated by:
questions.explanation: Slow (thermal) neutrons have much higher fission cross-section for ²³⁵U than fast neutrons.

questions.text: The half-life of a radioactive element is 20 minutes. After 1 hour, the fraction of the original amount remaining is:
questions.explanation: 1 hour = 3 half-lives. Fraction remaining = (1/2)³ = 1/8.

questions.text: For a common base transistor amplifier, the relationship between current gain α and β is:
questions.explanation: α = IC/IE; β = IC/IB. Since IE = IC + IB, α = β/(1+β).

questions.text: Bernoulli's principle is based on:
questions.explanation: Bernoulli's equation (P + ½ρv² + ρgh = const) is conservation of energy for fluid flow.

questions.text: The speed of sound in air at 0°C is 332 m/s. Its speed at 100°C is approximately:
Beats are produced when two sound waves have:
questions.explanation: Beats arise from superposition of waves of slightly different frequencies. Beat frequency = |f₁ − f₂|.

questions.text: The terminal velocity of a spherical body of radius r falling through a viscous fluid is proportional to:
questions.explanation: By Stokes' law: v_t = 2r²(ρ−σ)g / (9η). Terminal velocity ∝ r².

questions.text: The bulk modulus of a perfectly rigid (incompressible) body is:
questions.explanation: Bulk modulus B = −P/(ΔV/V). For incompressible material ΔV → 0, so B → ∞.

questions.text: When a transverse wave travels through a medium, the motion of individual particles is:

options.text: Along the direction of wave propagation
Perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation
At 45° to the direction of propagation

questions.explanation: In transverse waves, particle displacement is perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation.

questions.text: The phenomenon of Doppler effect is observed when there is relative motion between:
questions.explanation: Doppler effect occurs whenever there is relative motion between the source and the observer.

questions.text: The surface tension of a liquid generally _____ with increasing temperature:
questions.explanation: Increasing temperature increases kinetic energy of molecules, reducing intermolecular attraction and thus surface tension.

sections.title: Chemistry - Section A
sections.description: Compulsory section. Attempt all 45 questions. +4 for correct, -1 for incorrect.

questions.text: According to Raoult's law, relative lowering of vapour pressure of a solution is equal to:
questions.explanation: Raoult's law: (P° − P)/P° = χ₂ (mole fraction of solute).

questions.text: 18 g of glucose (MW = 180 g/mol) is dissolved in 200 g of water. Given Kb(water) = 0.52 K·kg/mol, the boiling point elevation is:
questions.explanation: Moles of glucose = 18/180 = 0.1 mol; m = 0.1/0.2 = 0.5 mol/kg; ΔTb = Kb×m = 0.52×0.5 = 0.26 K.

questions.text: Among the following equimolar aqueous solutions, the one with the HIGHEST osmotic pressure is:
questions.explanation: Osmotic pressure ∝ total solute particles. Na₃PO₄ → 4 ions (3Na⁺ + PO₄³⁻), giving highest particle count.

questions.text: The Van't Hoff factor (i) for a 0.01 M solution of K₂SO₄ (assuming complete dissociation) is:
questions.explanation: K₂SO₄ → 2K⁺ + SO₄²⁻, giving 3 ions. Therefore i = 3.

questions.text: Henry's law states that at constant temperature, the solubility of a gas in a liquid is:

options.text: Directly proportional to partial pressure of the gas

questions.explanation: Henry's law: p = KH × x, where x is mole fraction in solution. Solubility ∝ partial pressure.

questions.text: The standard Gibbs free energy change (ΔG°) and standard EMF (E°) of a cell are related by:
questions.explanation: ΔG° = −nFE°. For a spontaneous cell reaction, E° > 0 and ΔG° < 0.

questions.text: During electrolysis of water, the gas liberated at the cathode is:
questions.explanation: At cathode: 2H⁺ + 2e⁻ → H₂. Hydrogen is liberated at cathode; oxygen at anode.

questions.text: Kohlrausch's law states that at infinite dilution, the molar conductivity of an electrolyte equals:

options.text: Sum of ionic conductivities of all ions

questions.explanation: Λ°m = ν₊λ₊° + ν₋λ₋°. At infinite dilution, ions act independently.

questions.text: The standard electrode potential of the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE) is:
questions.explanation: By convention, the standard electrode potential of SHE is defined as exactly 0.00 V.

questions.text: The number of Faradays of electricity needed to deposit one mole of copper from a CuSO₄ solution (Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu) is:
questions.explanation: Each Cu²⁺ ion requires 2 electrons; so 1 mole Cu requires 2 × 96500 C = 2 F.

questions.text: The cell reaction at the anode (oxidation electrode) in an electrochemical cell always involves:
questions.explanation: Anode is the electrode where oxidation occurs (loss of electrons).

questions.text: Among Cu, Ag, Fe, and Au, which metal can displace hydrogen from dilute H₂SO₄?
questions.explanation: Fe lies above H in the electrochemical activity series and can displace H₂ from dilute acids.

questions.text: For a first-order reaction, the half-life t₁/₂ is given by:
questions.explanation: For first-order: t₁/₂ = ln2/k = 0.693/k. It is independent of initial concentration.

questions.text: The unit of rate constant for a second-order reaction is:
questions.explanation: For nth order: units of k = L^(n−1) mol^(1−n) s⁻¹. For n=2: L mol⁻¹ s⁻¹.

questions.text: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by:
questions.explanation: A catalyst provides an alternative pathway with lower activation energy, increasing reaction rate.

questions.text: If the activation energy of a reaction is zero, then the rate constant is:

options.text: Equal to Arrhenius frequency factor A

questions.explanation: k = Ae^(−Ea/RT). If Ea = 0, then k = Ae⁰ = A. Rate is independent of temperature.

questions.text: The complex [Fe(CN)₆]³⁻ contains Fe in the +3 oxidation state. With CN⁻ as strong-field ligand, the number of unpaired electrons is:
questions.explanation: Fe³⁺ is d⁵. With strong-field CN⁻ in octahedral field: configuration is t₂g⁵eg⁰ → 1 unpaired electron.

questions.text: The complex K₂[PtCl₄] is square planar. This is because Pt²⁺ is a _____ ion.
questions.explanation: d⁸ metal ions (like Pt²⁺, Pd²⁺, Au³⁺, Ni²⁺ with strong ligands) form square planar complexes.

questions.text: Which of the following complexes shows optical isomerism?
questions.explanation: [Co(en)₃]³⁺ is a chiral complex with no plane of symmetry; it exists as Δ and Λ enantiomers.

questions.text: The CFSE for an octahedral d³ complex in a weak crystal field is:
questions.explanation: d³ in octahedral (t₂g³eg⁰): CFSE = 3 × (−0.4Δo) = −1.2 Δo. Same in weak and strong field for d³.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of the complex [Co(en)₂Cl₂]Cl is:
questions.explanation: Co has coordination number 6 (2en + 2Cl in complex). Charge on complex = 3+ (from outer Cl⁻), so Co is +3.

questions.text: The product of acidic dehydration of propan-2-ol at 170°C is:
questions.explanation: Dehydration of propan-2-ol with conc. H₂SO₄ at 170°C gives propene (CH₃CH=CH₂) via E1 elimination.

questions.text: Which reagent is used to convert benzene to nitrobenzene?
questions.explanation: Nitration of benzene uses a nitrating mixture (conc. HNO₃ + conc. H₂SO₄) generating the electrophile NO₂⁺.

questions.text: Lucas test (ZnCl₂/conc. HCl) distinguishes between:
questions.explanation: Lucas test: 3° alcohol gives immediate turbidity; 2° gives turbidity in ~5 min; 1° shows no turbidity at room temp.

questions.text: Tollens' reagent (ammoniacal silver nitrate) gives a silver mirror test with:
questions.explanation: Tollens' reagent oxidises aldehydes (RCHO → RCOO⁻), forming silver mirror. Ketones are not oxidised.

questions.text: The reaction of acetaldehyde (CH₃CHO) with HCN gives:
questions.explanation: HCN adds across C=O (nucleophilic addition) to give a cyanohydrin: CH₃CHO + HCN → CH₃CH(OH)CN.

questions.text: Among the following, which compound undergoes aldol condensation?
questions.explanation: Aldol condensation requires α-hydrogen atoms. Only CH₃CHO has α-H atoms (on the CH₃ group).

questions.text: The product of the reaction of aniline (C₆H₅NH₂) with acetic anhydride is:
questions.explanation: C₆H₅NH₂ + (CH₃CO)₂O → C₆H₅NHCOCH₃ (acetanilide) + CH₃COOH.

questions.text: The total number of σ bonds in a benzene (C₆H₆) molecule is:
questions.explanation: Benzene has 6 C−H σ bonds and 6 C−C σ bonds (the ring), totalling 12 σ bonds.

questions.text: The hybridisation of carbon in CO₂ is:
questions.explanation: Carbon in CO₂ forms 2 double bonds (O=C=O), using sp hybridisation with linear geometry.

questions.text: Among N₂, O₂, F₂, which has the highest bond order?

options.text: All have the same bond order

questions.explanation: N₂ has a triple bond (bond order 3), making it the strongest and shortest of the three.

questions.text: VSEPR theory predicts that NH₃ has a _____ structure:
questions.explanation: NH₃ has 3 bonding pairs and 1 lone pair (sp³). The lone pair pushes bonds down, giving trigonal pyramidal geometry.

questions.text: Which of the following molecules has zero dipole moment?
questions.explanation: BF₃ is trigonal planar (sp²); symmetric arrangement causes bond dipoles to cancel → zero net dipole moment.

questions.text: Hess's law of constant heat summation is based on:

options.text: Conservation of energy (First law of thermodynamics)

questions.explanation: Hess's law states enthalpy change is independent of path (state function), based on conservation of energy.

questions.text: For a process to be spontaneous at constant temperature and pressure:
questions.explanation: At constant T and P, ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. Spontaneous processes have ΔG < 0.

questions.text: 12 g of carbon (MW = 12) burns completely in excess O₂. Given ΔcH°(C) = −393.5 kJ/mol, heat released is:
questions.explanation: 12 g of carbon = 1 mol. Heat released = 1 × 393.5 = 393.5 kJ.

questions.text: For the equilibrium N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g), increasing pressure will shift equilibrium:
questions.explanation: 4 moles of gas → 2 moles of gas. Increasing pressure favours the side with fewer moles of gas (forward reaction).

questions.text: The relationship between Kp and Kc for a gaseous equilibrium with Δn moles of gas is:
questions.explanation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn, where Δn = moles of gaseous products − moles of gaseous reactants.

questions.text: For the reaction H₂(g) + I₂(g) ⇌ 2HI(g), the relationship between Kp and Kc is:
questions.explanation: Δn = 2 − (1+1) = 0. Therefore Kp = Kc(RT)⁰ = Kc.

questions.text: Nylon-6,6 is a condensation polymer formed from:
questions.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is made from hexamethylenediamine [H₂N(CH₂)₆NH₂] and adipic acid [HOOC(CH₂)₄COOH].

questions.text: Natural rubber is a polymer of:
questions.explanation: Natural rubber is cis-1,4-polyisoprene, a polymer of isoprene (CH₂=C(CH₃)−CH=CH₂).

questions.text: The primary structure of a protein refers to:

options.text: The sequence of amino acids linked by peptide bonds

questions.explanation: Primary structure = the linear sequence of amino acids connected by peptide bonds.

questions.text: Which vitamin is essential for blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Vitamin K is required for the synthesis of prothrombin and other clotting factors.

sections.title: Chemistry - Section B
sections.description: Attempt any 10 out of 15 questions. Only your best 10 answers will be scored.

questions.text: The Bohr model of the atom could NOT successfully explain:
questions.explanation: Bohr's model works only for one-electron species (H, He⁺, Li²⁺). It fails for multi-electron atoms.

questions.text: An electron in a 4f subshell has quantum numbers:
questions.explanation: For f subshell, l = 3. So 4f: n = 4, l = 3.

questions.text: Heisenberg's uncertainty principle states:
questions.explanation: The uncertainty principle: Δx·Δp ≥ ħ/2 = h/4π. Position and momentum cannot both be precisely determined.

questions.text: Across a period in the periodic table, ionisation enthalpy generally:
questions.explanation: Increasing nuclear charge across a period holds electrons more tightly, raising ionisation enthalpy.

questions.text: Among the halogens, the element with the highest electron affinity is:
questions.explanation: Cl has higher electron affinity than F because F's small size causes strong electron-electron repulsion when an electron is added.

questions.text: The highest oxidation state exhibited by any first-row transition metal in its compounds is:
questions.explanation: Mn shows +7 in KMnO₄ (potassium permanganate), the highest oxidation state in first row d-block elements.

questions.text: Which of the following d-block ion is diamagnetic?
questions.explanation: Zn²⁺ has d¹⁰ configuration (all electrons paired) → diamagnetic. All others have unpaired electrons.

questions.text: XeF₂ has which molecular geometry?
questions.explanation: Xe in XeF₂ has 5 electron pairs (3 lone pairs + 2 bonding pairs), giving trigonal bipyramidal electron geometry but linear molecular geometry.

questions.text: Phosphorus exists as P₄ molecules (rather than P₂) because:

options.text: P has high electronegativity like N
P is too large for stable pπ–pπ double bonds

questions.explanation: Unlike N, P cannot form stable pπ–pπ bonds due to its large atomic size, so it forms P₄ with single bonds instead.

questions.text: Among alkaline earth metals, which does NOT react with water under normal conditions?
questions.explanation: Be does not react with water due to a protective oxide layer and high charge density.

questions.text: When sodium burns in excess oxygen, the product formed is:
questions.explanation: Na burns in excess O₂ to give Na₂O₂. Only K, Rb, Cs form superoxides (MO₂) with excess O₂.

questions.text: Lanthanoid contraction occurs due to:

options.text: Increase in nuclear charge without effective shielding by 4f electrons
Decrease in nuclear charge across the series
Increase in atomic radius across the series

questions.explanation: 4f electrons shield the nuclear charge poorly from outer electrons. As nuclear charge increases across lanthanoids, atomic size decreases.

questions.text: Cl₂ reacts with cold dilute NaOH to give:
questions.explanation: Cl₂ + 2NaOH(cold) → NaCl + NaOCl + H₂O. Hot concentrated NaOH gives NaClO₃.

questions.text: The primary gas responsible for depletion of the stratospheric ozone layer is:
questions.explanation: CFCs release Cl· radicals in the stratosphere, which catalytically destroy ozone: Cl· + O₃ → ClO· + O₂.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: N₂ is a non-polar, diatomic molecule that does not absorb IR radiation and is not a greenhouse gas.

sections.title: Biology - Section A
sections.description: Compulsory section. Attempt all 45 questions. +4 for correct, -1 for incorrect.

questions.text: Which organelle is responsible for the synthesis of lipids?
questions.explanation: Smooth ER (SER) is the primary site of lipid and steroid synthesis in the cell.

questions.text: The fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Singer and Nicolson (1972) proposed the fluid mosaic model, describing the membrane as a fluid bilayer with embedded proteins.

questions.text: Plasmodesmata are:

options.text: Cytoplasmic connections between adjacent plant cells

questions.explanation: Plasmodesmata are cytoplasmic bridges that pass through cell walls, connecting neighbouring plant cells.

questions.text: 70S ribosomes are found in:
questions.explanation: 70S ribosomes are found in prokaryotes and in endosymbiotic organelles (mitochondria and chloroplasts) of eukaryotes.

questions.text: Which organelle is called the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP through oxidative phosphorylation, earning the name 'powerhouse of the cell'.

questions.text: Crossing over during meiosis occurs in which substage of prophase I?
questions.explanation: Crossing over (exchange of genetic material between non-sister chromatids) occurs during pachytene of prophase I.

questions.text: Colchicine arrests cell division at metaphase by:
questions.explanation: Colchicine binds tubulin and prevents polymerisation of microtubules, disrupting spindle formation.

questions.text: The number of chromosomes in a human cell immediately after meiosis I is:
questions.explanation: After meiosis I, the cell is haploid with 23 chromosomes (each still consisting of 2 sister chromatids).

questions.text: Synapsis of homologous chromosomes occurs during:
questions.explanation: Synapsis (pairing of homologous chromosomes to form bivalents) occurs during zygotene of prophase I.

questions.text: The enzyme that unwinds the DNA double helix during replication is:
questions.explanation: Helicase breaks hydrogen bonds between base pairs, unwinding the double helix at the replication fork.

questions.text: The start codon AUG codes for the amino acid:
questions.explanation: AUG is the universal start codon and codes for methionine (Met/M). All polypeptides begin with Met.

questions.text: Post-transcriptional modification of hnRNA does NOT include:

options.text: Methylation of adenine residues within the coding sequence

questions.explanation: hnRNA processing includes 5' capping, 3' poly-A tail addition, and splicing. Adenine methylation in coding sequences is not a standard modification.

questions.text: The template strand used during transcription is also called the:
questions.explanation: The template strand (3'→5') is also called the antisense or non-coding strand. The non-template strand has the same sequence as mRNA.

questions.text: In the lac operon, the inducer molecule that prevents the repressor from binding to the operator is:
questions.explanation: Allolactose (formed from lactose) binds the lac repressor, causing it to dissociate from the operator, allowing transcription.

questions.text: Mendel's law of independent assortment does NOT apply when:

options.text: Genes are on the same chromosome (genetic linkage)
One allele is dominant over the other

questions.explanation: Linked genes (on the same chromosome) do not assort independently; they tend to be inherited together.

questions.text: A woman who is a carrier for colourblindness (X-linked recessive, XᶜX) marries a normal man (XY). The probability of having a colourblind child is:
questions.explanation: Offspring: XᶜX (carrier daughter), XX (normal daughter), XᶜY (colourblind son), XY (normal son). 1 in 4 = 1/4 chance.

questions.text: A test cross is performed by crossing the organism of unknown genotype with a:
questions.explanation: In a test cross, the unknown genotype is crossed with a recessive homozygote (aa) to reveal which alleles are present.

questions.text: Down syndrome (Trisomy 21) is characterised by having _____ chromosomes in somatic cells:
questions.explanation: Down syndrome results from trisomy of chromosome 21 (2n + 1 = 47).

questions.text: Turner syndrome (45, XO) is characterised by:

options.text: Male phenotype with extra X chromosome
Female phenotype, sterile, webbed neck, short stature

questions.explanation: Turner syndrome (45,XO): female with short stature, webbed neck, sterile, underdeveloped secondary sex characters.

questions.text: A Y-chromosome linked (holandric) trait is transmitted:

options.text: From father to all sons only
From father to all daughters only

questions.explanation: Holandric genes are on the Y chromosome and are transmitted from father to ALL sons (never to daughters).

questions.text: The enzyme that converts pepsinogen into pepsin is:
questions.explanation: HCl in gastric juice activates pepsinogen to pepsin by autocatalytic cleavage. Pepsin then continues activating more pepsinogen.

questions.text: Bile does NOT contain:
questions.explanation: Bile contains bile salts, bile pigments, cholesterol, and water. It does NOT contain digestive enzymes; it emulsifies fats.

questions.text: The final products of complete protein digestion absorbed in the small intestine are:
questions.explanation: Proteins are fully hydrolysed to individual amino acids by proteases (pepsin, trypsin, chymotrypsin, peptidases).

questions.text: Digestion of starch begins in the:
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase (ptyalin) in the mouth starts breaking down starch into maltose.

questions.text: The pacemaker of the human heart is the:
questions.explanation: The SA node generates the primary rhythmic electrical impulse, setting the heart rate (~72 beats/min).

questions.text: The blood vessel that carries oxygenated blood from the lungs to the left atrium is the:
questions.explanation: Pulmonary veins carry oxygenated blood from the lungs to the left atrium. Pulmonary arteries carry deoxygenated blood to lungs.

questions.text: Normal blood pressure in a healthy adult is approximately:
questions.explanation: Normal blood pressure is 120 mmHg systolic (heart contracts) / 80 mmHg diastolic (heart relaxes).

questions.text: The volume of air inspired or expired in each normal breath at rest is called:
questions.explanation: Tidal volume (~500 mL) is the air exchanged in one normal, quiet breath.

questions.text: The major form in which CO₂ is transported in the blood is as:
questions.explanation: About 70% of CO₂ is carried as bicarbonate ions (HCO₃⁻); ~23% as carbaminohaemoglobin; ~7% dissolved.

questions.text: The cells that produce surfactant in the lungs are:
questions.explanation: Type II pneumocytes secrete surfactant (dipalmitoyl phosphatidylcholine), which reduces alveolar surface tension.

questions.text: The juxtaglomerular apparatus (JGA) secretes the enzyme:
questions.explanation: JGA cells (granular cells) secrete renin when blood pressure falls, initiating the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone cascade.

questions.text: The countercurrent multiplier mechanism in the kidney loop of Henle creates:

options.text: A gradient of decreasing osmolarity from cortex to medulla
A gradient of increasing osmolarity from cortex to medulla

questions.explanation: The loop of Henle creates a hyperosmotic medullary interstitium (increasing osmolarity toward the inner medulla), enabling concentration of urine.

questions.text: Urea is primarily synthesised in the:
questions.explanation: Urea is produced in the liver via the ornithine cycle (urea cycle) from ammonia and CO₂.

questions.text: The glomerular filtration rate (GFR) in a healthy adult is approximately:
questions.explanation: GFR ≈ 125 mL/min (or 180 L/day). Most filtrate is reabsorbed; ~1.5 L urine is excreted per day.

questions.text: The neurotransmitter released at the neuromuscular junction is:
questions.explanation: Acetylcholine (ACh) is released from motor neuron terminals at the neuromuscular junction, triggering muscle contraction.

questions.text: Insulin is produced by which cells of the islets of Langerhans?
questions.explanation: Beta cells secrete insulin. Alpha cells secrete glucagon. Delta cells secrete somatostatin.

questions.text: The hormone that stimulates uterine contractions during labour is:
questions.explanation: Oxytocin, released from the posterior pituitary, causes uterine contractions during parturition and milk ejection.

questions.text: Adrenaline (epinephrine) is secreted by:
questions.explanation: Adrenal medulla (chromaffin cells) secretes adrenaline and noradrenaline as part of the 'fight-or-flight' response.

questions.text: The site of light-dependent reactions (light reactions) in photosynthesis is the:
questions.explanation: Light reactions occur on the thylakoid membranes. The Calvin cycle (dark reactions) occurs in the stroma.

questions.text: Photorespiration involves which organelles in C3 plants?
questions.explanation: Photorespiration begins in chloroplasts (RuBisCO oxygenase activity), continues in peroxisomes, and is completed in mitochondria.

questions.text: The plant hormone responsible for causing apical dominance is:
questions.explanation: High auxin concentrations from the apical meristem inhibit growth of lateral buds, causing apical dominance.

questions.text: C4 plants (like maize and sugarcane) are more efficient in photosynthesis in hot, sunny conditions because:

options.text: They concentrate CO₂ around RuBisCO, minimising photorespiration

questions.explanation: C4 plants fix CO₂ first via PEP carboxylase in mesophyll, then concentrate CO₂ in bundle sheath cells, suppressing RuBisCO oxygenase activity.

questions.text: Anaerobic respiration in yeast produces:
questions.explanation: Yeast fermentation: Pyruvate → Acetaldehyde → Ethanol + CO₂ (in the absence of O₂).

questions.text: Vernalisation is the process by which:

options.text: Seeds germinate after stratification with cold
Plants are induced to flower following prolonged exposure to cold temperatures
Leaves grow in response to blue light

questions.explanation: Vernalisation is the acceleration of flowering in plants by exposure to cold temperatures (e.g., winter wheat requires cold to flower in spring).

sections.title: Biology - Section B
sections.description: Attempt any 10 out of 15 questions. Only your best 10 answers will be scored.

questions.text: Restriction endonucleases cut DNA at:
questions.explanation: Restriction enzymes recognise specific palindromic sequences (e.g., EcoRI recognises GAATTC) and cut at or near these sites.

questions.text: PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) requires the enzyme:
questions.explanation: Taq polymerase (from Thermus aquaticus) is thermostable and survives repeated heating cycles in PCR.

questions.text: Ti plasmid used in plant genetic engineering is derived from:
questions.explanation: Ti (tumour-inducing) plasmid from A. tumefaciens can integrate T-DNA into the plant genome, making it a natural vector.

questions.text: Gel electrophoresis separates DNA fragments based on:

options.text: Size (number of base pairs) — smaller fragments migrate faster

questions.explanation: In agarose gel electrophoresis, all DNA fragments have similar charge/mass ratios; separation is by size. Smaller fragments migrate farther.

questions.text: Double fertilisation in angiosperms results in:

options.text: A diploid embryo and a triploid endosperm

questions.explanation: One sperm fuses with egg → diploid embryo; the other sperm fuses with 2 polar nuclei → triploid (3n) endosperm.

questions.text: The corpus luteum, after ovulation, primarily secretes:
questions.explanation: Corpus luteum (from ruptured follicle) secretes progesterone, which maintains the endometrium for implantation.

questions.text: Which feature is NOT characteristic of Kingdom Fungi?
questions.explanation: Fungi are heterotrophs (saprophytes, parasites, or symbionts). They lack chlorophyll and cannot perform photosynthesis.

questions.text: Viroids differ from viruses in that viroids:
questions.explanation: Viroids consist only of small, circular ssRNA with no protein coat. They cause plant diseases (e.g., potato spindle tuber disease).

questions.text: Mycorrhiza is a mutualistic association between:

options.text: Fungi and roots of vascular plants

questions.explanation: Mycorrhiza is a symbiotic relationship between soil fungi and plant roots; fungi aid mineral absorption and plant provides carbohydrates.

questions.text: The climax community in ecological succession is best described as:

options.text: A stable, self-sustaining community in equilibrium with the environment
A community with maximum species diversity always

questions.explanation: The climax community is the final stable state of succession, in equilibrium with local climate and soil.

questions.text: Lichens are considered an example of:
questions.explanation: Lichens are a mutualistic association between fungi and photosynthetic partners (algae or cyanobacteria). Both benefit.

questions.text: Which provides the most direct evidence for biological evolution?
questions.explanation: Fossil records provide direct, chronological evidence of life forms from different geological periods, showing evolutionary changes over time.

questions.text: The theory of natural selection as the mechanism of evolution was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Darwin proposed natural selection in 'On the Origin of Species' (1859). De Vries proposed mutation theory.

questions.text: The flippers of penguins and dolphins are an example of:
questions.explanation: Penguin and dolphin flippers have different evolutionary origins but similar function — convergent evolution producing analogous structures.

questions.text: Biopiracy refers to:

options.text: Piracy of biodiversity data from public databases
Unauthorised use of biological resources or traditional knowledge from biodiverse regions without benefit sharing
Collection of specimens without scientific permits

questions.explanation: Biopiracy is the exploitation of biological resources and associated traditional knowledge of indigenous communities without fair compensation.
